films.title: Amélie
films.cast: Audrey Tautou, Mathieu Kassovitz
films.description: Enjoy this classic, swooningly romantic French hit on the big screen as it celebrates its 25th anniversary.

films.title: All About Eve
films.cast: Bette Davis, Anne Baxter, Marilyn Monroe, Celeste Holm
films.description: A devoted fan claws her way into Broadway’s inner sanctum, exposing how ambition, insecurity and performance blur on stage and off in this venomously witty classic.

films.title: An American Tail
films.description: A young mouse must follow his family on an epic journey to America in this celebrated animation.

films.title: Another Day, Another Man + Elevator Girls in Bondage
films.description: A sexploitation classic and a rare, queer underground film make for an unforgettable double-bill.

tags: Trash! The Wildest Films You’ve Ever Seen

films.title: Bad Timing
films.cast: Art Garfunkel, Theresa Russell, Harvey Keitel
films.description: Nicolas Roeg pushes his non-linear approach to storytelling with this enigmatic mystery that was later heralded as one of the finest films of the 1980s.

films.title: Bait
films.cast: Edward Rowe, Mary Woodvine, Simon Shepherd
films.description: The debut film from Cornish auteur Mark Jenkin explores gentrification through 16mm monochrome.

films.title: Bait
films.cast: Edward Rowe, Mary Woodvine, Simon Shepherd
films.description: The debut film from Cornish auteur Mark Jenkin explores gentrification through 16mm monochrome.

films.title: Bad Timing
films.cast: Art Garfunkel, Theresa Russell, Harvey Keitel
films.description: Nicolas Roeg pushes his non-linear approach to storytelling with this enigmatic mystery that was later heralded as one of the finest films of the 1980s.

films.title: Meet the Projectionists
films.description: Learn about what goes on behind the scenes at BFI Southbank and BFI IMAX.

films.title: Being John Malkovich
films.cast: John Cusack, Cameron Diaz, Catherine Keener, John Malkovich
films.description: A secret doorway into a movie star’s mind turns fantasy into moral chaos in this deliriously inventive satire of identity, ambition and control.

films.title: Blade II
films.cast: Wesley Snipes, Kris Kristofferson, Ron Perlman, Leonor Varela
films.description: A visceral comic book adaptation executed with flare and originality.

films.title: Black God, White Devil
films.cast: Geraldo Del Rey, Yoná Magalhães, Othon Bastos
films.description: A restored Cinema Novo landmark where faith, banditry and revolt collide.

films.title: The Blue Trail
films.cast: Denis Weinberg, Rodrigo Santoro, Miriam Socarrás
films.description: Gabriel Mascaro’s wondrous adventure of a defiant elder fleeing an ageist society is a life-affirming boat-movie that manages laughs and warmth in the face of calamity.

films.title: The Bounty
films.cast: Anthony Hopkins, Mel Gibson, Laurence Olivier, Daniel Day-Lewis
films.description: This striking version of the mutiny that took place on the Bounty features an incredible British cast, including Anthony Hopkins and Laurence Olivier.

films.title: The Boxer
films.cast: Daniel Day-Lewis, Emily Watson, Brian Cox
films.description: Jim Sheridan’s powerful 1997 drama about love, boxing and redemption unfolds against the backdrop of the Irish Troubles.

films.title: Bride of Frankenstein
films.cast: Boris Karloff, Elsa Lanchester, Colin Clive
films.description: Boris Karloff is back as the Monster in one of cinema’s great sequels.

films.title: Bye Bye Brazil
films.cast: José Wilker, Betty Faria, Fábio Jr
films.description: A bittersweet journey through a nation where modernity erases popular traditions.

films.title: Cinema Novo
films.description: An archival immersion into the ideas, images and urgency of Cinema Novo.

films.title: Chuck Billy and the Marvelous Guava Tree
films.cast: Isaac Amendoim, Pedro Dantas, Anna Julia Dias
films.description: A young boy decides to protect a beloved tree from an avaricious property developer.

films.title: The Conversation
films.cast: Gene Hackman, John Cazale
films.description: Between his first two Godfather films, Francis Ford Coppola produced one of the defining paranoid thrillers of the 1970s, featuring one of Gene Hackman’s finest performances.

films.title: City of God
films.cast: Alexandre Rodrigues, Leandro Firmino, Jonathan Haagensen, Douglas Silva
films.description: An explosive favela chronicle that transformed Brazilian cinema worldwide.

films.title: The Conversation
films.cast: Gene Hackman, John Cazale
films.description: Between his first two Godfather films, Francis Ford Coppola produced one of the defining paranoid thrillers of the 1970s, featuring one of Gene Hackman’s finest performances.

films.title: Cotton Queen
films.cast: Mihad Murtada, Rabha Mohamed Mahmoud
films.description: In Suzannah Mirghani’s feature debut, folklore and colonial resistance take centre stage in a Sudanese village.

films.title: Creative Minds of Tomorrow
films.description: An event aimed at promoting the next generation of filmmaking talent.

films.title: Crimson Peak
films.cast: Mia Wasikowska, Tom Hiddleston, Jessica Chastain, Charlie Hunnam
films.description: An under-appreciated gothic gem ripe for reappraisal and a companion piece to Frankenstein.

films.title: Cronos
films.cast: Federico Luppi, Ron Perlman, Claudio Brook, Margarita Isabel
films.description: Del Toro’s debut is a stylish, original and politically potent vampire fable.

films.title: Cronos
films.cast: Federico Luppi, Ron Perlman, Claudio Brook, Margarita Isabel
films.description: Del Toro’s debut is a stylish, original and politically potent vampire fable.

films.title: Cronos
films.cast: Federico Luppi, Ron Perlman, Claudio Brook, Margarita Isabel
films.description: Del Toro’s debut is a stylish, original and politically potent vampire fable.

films.title: Cronos
films.cast: Federico Luppi, Ron Perlman, Claudio Brook, Margarita Isabel
films.description: Del Toro’s debut is a stylish, original and politically potent vampire fable.

films.title: David Lowery in Conversation
films.description: In his latest film, Mother Mary, Anne Hathaway stars as the eponymous pop star and Michaela Coel as her former confidant and fashion designer. Join us to learn more and celebrate Lowery’s work and career so far.

films.title: Days and Nights in the Forest
films.cast: Soumitra Chatterjee, Subhendu Chatterjee, Sharmila Tagore
films.description: Four friends embark on a countryside holiday that will transform them in this glorious gem from Satyajit Ray.

films.title: The Devil’s Backbone
films.cast: Federico Luppi, Marisa Paredes, Eduardo Noriega, Fernando Tielve
films.description: A provocative and acclaimed synthesis of historical melodrama, murder mystery and gothic ghost tale.

films.title: Dolores
films.cast: Carla Ribas, Ariane Aparecida, Naruna Costa, Zezé Motta
films.description: A stunning central performance dominates this character study of a woman who suspects her life is about to undergo seismic change.

films.title: The Drama
films.cast: Zendaya, Robert Pattinson, Alana Haim and Mamoudou Athie
films.description: Zendaya and Robert Pattinson star as nearly-newlyweds whose lives unravel during the week of their wedding, in Kristoffer Borgli’s electric black comedy.

films.title: Enys Men
films.cast: Mary Woodvine, Edward Rowe, Flo Crowe
films.description: Mary Woodvine stars in this rural mind-bending folk horror, the second of Mark Jenkin’s Cornish Trilogy.

films.title: The Fall of the House of Usher
films.cast: Marguerite Gance, Jean Debucourt, Charles Lamy
films.description: Jean Epstein transforms Edgar Allen Poe’s tale into a haunting reverie. It’s accompanied by a collection of equally transcendent shorts.

films.title: Enys Men
films.cast: Mary Woodvine, Edward Rowe, Flo Crowe
films.description: Mary Woodvine stars in this rural mind-bending folk horror, the second of Mark Jenkin’s Cornish Trilogy.

films.title: The Fallen Idol
films.cast: Ralph Richardson, Michèle Morgan, Sonia Dresdel, Bobby Henrey
films.description: A canonical classic from the team behind The Third Man.

films.title: Farewell to Spring
films.cast: Keiji Sada, Ineko Arima
films.description: Keisuke Kinoshita’s achingly beautiful film is about the intense feelings between five young men who return to their Aizu hometown.

films.title: Festival of Britain: Through the Eyes of the Archive
films.description: See how the BFI National Archive has captured the Festival of Britain.

films.title: The Fighter
films.cast: Mark Wahlberg, Christian Bale, Amy Adams, Melissa Leo
films.description: David O. Russell directs a raw, Academy Award-winning biopic of the Lowell fighter ‘Irish’ Micky Ward.

films.title: Fighters
films.description: Ron Peck’s gritty and enduring documentary reveals the realities of the everyday boxing world.

films.title: Fly Away Home
films.cast: Anna Paquin, Jeff Daniels, Dana Delany
films.description: Young Amy raises a family of goslings but must face some home truths as she does so in this heartwarming family drama.

films.title: A Fish in the Bathtub
films.cast: Jerry Stiller, Anne Meara, Mark Ruffalo, Jane Adams
films.description: A bathtub-bound fish becomes the final straw in a forty-year marriage, as Jerry Stiller and Anne Meara spar their way through love, habit and survival.

films.title: The Flying Scot
films.cast: Lee Patterson, Kay Callard, Alan Gifford
films.description: This is British low-budget crime cinema at its very best.

films.title: Foreign Land
films.cast: Fernando Alves Pinto, Fernanda Torres, Alexandre Borges
films.description: Exile and noir romance in the film that relaunched Brazilian cinema.

films.title: Future Forward: The Evolving Landscape of India-UK Co-productions, a UK Asian Film Festival Initiative
films.description: An opportunity to find out about schemes aimed at supporting new film projects.

films.title: Free Nelson Mandela
films.description: The story behind the remarkable release of Nelson Mandela, following decades in captivity.

films.title: Gallivant
films.cast: Andrew Kötting, Eden Kötting, Benji Ming, Gladys Morris
films.description: Andrew Kötting’s most celebrated film, a journey around the nooks and crannies of British coastal communities, is also a loving portrait of his grandmother and daughter.

films.title: Gattaca
films.cast: Ethan Hawke, Uma Thurman, Jude Law, Alan Arkin
films.description: Ethan Hawke, Uma Thurman and Jude Law head an impressive, intelligent sci-fi whose exploration of DNA manipulation feels frighteningly real almost three decades after its first release.

films.title: The Gold Rush
films.cast: Georgina Hale, Mack Swain, Tom Murray
films.description: A welcome restoration of Chaplin’s beloved comedy, which finds his Tramp seeking his fortune in the Klondike.

films.title: Green Card
films.cast: Gérard Depardieu, Andie MacDowell, Bebe Neuwirth
films.description: Peter Weir’s most gentle film is a delightful romcom that refines the definition of ‘opposites attract’.

tags: Finding Your Way: The Films of Peter Weir

films.title: Guillermo del Toro Sip and Paint
films.description: Let the artistic visions of Guillermo del Toro inspire you with this creative, collaborative painting session.

films.title: Guillermo del Toro Quiz
films.description: Join us for a quiz celebrating del Toro’s unique cinematic visions.

films.title: The Happiest Days of Your Life
films.cast: Alastair Sim, Margaret Rutherford, Joyce Grenfell, Richard Wattis
films.description: Alastair Sim and Margaret Rutherford, in disarmingly excellent form, navigate the delicate predicament of gender mix-up.

films.title: The Happiest Days of Your Life
films.cast: Alastair Sim, Margaret Rutherford, Joyce Grenfell, Richard Wattis
films.description: Alastair Sim and Margaret Rutherford, in disarmingly excellent form, navigate the delicate predicament of gender mix-up.

films.title: Hell Is a City
films.cast: Stanley Baker, John Crawford, Billie Whitelaw, Donald Pleasence
films.description: Stanley Baker’s brooding inspector shines darkly in an existentialist police procedure drama.

films.title: Hellboy II: The Golden Army
films.cast: Ron Perlman, Selma Blair, Doug Jones, Jeffrey Tambor
films.description: Another Mike Mignola collaboration that offers thrills, meticulous design and entertainment.

films.title: Hellboy
films.cast: Ron Perlman, John Hurt, Selma Blair, Doug Jones
films.description: A career highlight that offers a neat spin on the beauty and the beast fable.

films.title: Hunted
films.cast: Dirk Bogarde, Jon Whiteley, Elizabeth Sellars, Kay Walsh
films.description: A cinematic milestone blending the anguish of film noir with the pathos of neorealism.

films.title: Hunted
films.cast: Dirk Bogarde, Jon Whiteley, Elizabeth Sellars, Kay Walsh
films.description: A cinematic milestone blending the anguish of film noir with the pathos of neorealism.

films.title: The Hurricane
films.cast: Denzel Washington, John Hannah, Vicellous Reon Shannon, Deborah Kara Unger
films.description: Norman Jewison’s moving biopic details the life of New Jersey boxer Rubin ‘the Hurricane’ Carter and his 20-year fight for justice.

films.title: Hollywood Shuffle
films.cast: Robert Townsend, Keenen Ivory Wayans, John Witherspoon
films.description: Breaking into Hollywood is easy. Keeping your dignity is the hard part. Experience Robert Townsend’s comedy classic in 35mm.

films.title: Inland Empire
films.cast: Laura Dern, Jeremy Irons, Justin Theroux
films.description: Arguably David Lynch’s strangest film since Eraserhead, his final feature is a nightmarish portrait of an actor whose role in a film sees her descend into madness.

films.title: Journeyman
films.cast: Paddy Considine, Jodie Whittaker, Paul Popplewell, Brendan Ingle
films.description: Paddy Considine writes, directs and stars in this powerful portrait of a boxer whose life is transformed after his body succumbs to the violence of the sport.

films.title: The Last Wave
films.cast: Richard Chamberlain, Olivia Hamnett, David Gulpilil
films.description: Striking imagery and a dreamlike atmosphere define this poetic reckoning with Australian history.

tags: Finding Your Way: The Films of Peter Weir

films.title: Jawbone
films.cast: Johnny Harris, Ray Winstone, Ian McShane
films.description: A former youth boxing champion, having hit rock bottom, returns to his childhood boxing club in Thomas Q. Napper’s powerful drama.

films.title: Inferno, Purgatory and Paradise: Ken Hollings’ Guide to the Trash Underworld
films.description: Ken Hollings takes our hand and leads us into the Trash Underworld in a special wide-ranging talk, drawing on his extraordinary Trash Trilogy book series.

films.title: Macho Dancer
films.cast: Allan Paule, Daniel Fernando
films.description: A young man travels to Manila to work as a dancer, stripper and rent boy in Lino Brocka’s acclaimed gay drama.

films.title: Lost in Translation
films.cast: Bill Murray, Scarlett Johansson, Giovanni Ribisi, Anna Faris
films.description: In a neon-lit city far from home, two lost souls stumble into each other, finding laughter, comfort and a fleeting escape from life’s disconnection.

films.title: Lower City
films.cast: Lázaro Ramos, Wagner Moura, Alice Braga
films.description: Desire and rivalry ignite in this raw, sensual Salvador drama.

films.title: Man Marked for Death, Twenty Years Later
films.description: A restored landmark where interrupted fiction becomes living memory.

films.title: Manas
films.cast: Jamilli Correa, Fátima Macedo, Rômulo Braga, Dira Paes
films.description: An Amazonian teenager challenges the violent structures shaping the lives of women around her.

films.title: Mandy
films.cast: Phyllis Calvert, Jack Hawkins, Terence Morgan, Mandy Miller
films.description: This shattering portrait of childhood is acted and directed with astonishing mastery.

films.title: The Margin
films.cast: Mario Benvenutti, Valéria Vidal, Bentinho
films.description: A riverbank reverie of outsiders – the defining vision of Cinema Marginal.

films.title: Mark Kermode Live in 3D at the BFI
films.description: The critic presents the latest episode of his live discussion, on stage in NFT1.

films.title: Archive Tour
films.description: Enjoy a tour through the inner workings of the BFI National Archive.

films.title: Mike Figgis in conversation with Mark Jenkin
films.description: Two bold and visionary filmmakers discuss their craft.

films.title: Mera Lyari
films.cast: Ayesha Omar, Dananeer Mobeen, Summaya Mumtaz
films.description: The story of two young women determined to pursue their football dreams.

films.title: Mimic: Director’s Cut
films.cast: Mira Sorvino, Jeremy Northam, Josh Brolin, Charles S. Dutton
films.description: A bruising experience for Guillermo del Toro, his first Hollywood outing nevertheless showcases his grand artistic vision and explores the dark side of scientific progress.

films.title: Naked Acts
films.cast: Jake-Ann Jones, Patricia DeArcy, John McKie, Ron Cephas Jones
films.description: Celebrated as a key film in the canon of African-American independent cinema in the 1990s, Bridgett M. Davis’s first and only feature is a fresh, sensitive exploration of a Black actress attempting to reclaim her sexuality.

films.title: Never Let Go
films.cast: Richard Todd, Peter Sellers, Elizabeth Sellars, Adam Faith
films.description: A tough and psychologically nuanced British take on The Bicycle Thieves.

films.title: New Writings from the UK Asian Film Festival
films.description: Writer, poet and filmmaker Mihir Chitre introduces Screenwriters Inc., his new book featuring conversations with leading Indian screenwriters on their creative processes.

films.title: Hitchcock & Herrmann – The Friendship & Film Scores that Changed Cinema
films.description: Join author Steven C. Smith and discover the iconic partnership of Bernard Herrmann and Alfred Hitchcock, which still resonates in pop culture today.

films.title: In the Scene: Agnès Varda
films.description: Author and film scholar Dr Paul Sutton presents his new book on Agnès Varda, which explores the filmmaker’s key films of in the context of her life.

films.title: Melodrama as Provocateur
films.description: Christine Gledhill presents Melodrama as Provocateur, exploring Linda Williams’s claim that Hollywood’s modernity rests on melodrama’s emotional power and optimism.

films.title: Nightmare Alley: Vision in Darkness and Light (Black & White version)
films.cast: Bradley Cooper, Cate Blanchett, Toni Collette, Rooney Mara
films.description: A noir about the dark of heart, this is hugely accomplished filmmaking whether in black and white or colour.

films.title: Nightmare Alley
films.cast: Bradley Cooper, Cate Blanchett, Toni Collette, Rooney Mara
films.description: A noir about the dark of heart, this is hugely accomplished filmmaking whether in black and white or colour.

films.title: Normal
films.cast: Bob Odenkirk, Henry Winkler, Lena Headey
films.description: A sheriff realises he doesn’t know his smalltown quite as well as he should, in this belter starring Bob Odenkirk and directed by Ben Wheatley.

films.title: UK Premiere of 4K Restoration: Not a Pretty Picture
films.cast: Michele Manenti, Jim Carrington, Anne Mundstuk
films.description: A radical act of self-examination, Not a Pretty Picture challenges how cinema can confront sexual violence – without exploiting it – and whether re-enactment can offer truth and healing or harm.

films.title: Open Endings
films.cast: Janella Salvador, Jasmine Curtis-Smith, Klea Pineda, Leanne Mamonong
films.description: This smart, endearing drama from the Philippines is about four queer women – exes who have become best friends.

films.title: Opening Night
films.cast: Gena Rowlands, Ben Gazzara, Joan Blondell, Paul Stewart
films.description: Haunted by death and terrified of ageing, a fragile star stumbles towards opening night, where grief, booze and brilliance collide – and the stage demands she survive it.

films.title: Opening Night
films.cast: Gena Rowlands, Ben Gazzara, Joan Blondell, Paul Stewart
films.description: Haunted by death and terrified of ageing, a fragile star stumbles towards opening night, where grief, booze and brilliance collide – and the stage demands she survive it.

films.title: A Page of Madness
films.cast: Masao Inoue, Yoshie Nakagawa, Ayako Iijima
films.description: A man takes a job at an asylum to be near his wife in this powerful cinematic vision of the human mind, followed by a series of pivotal, silent avant-garde shorts.

films.title: Pickpocket
films.cast: Martin LaSalle, Pierre Leymarie, Marika Green
films.description: Robert Bresson’s minimal approach to human drama finds its perfect subject in this perfectly rendered tale of a Parisian cutpurse.

films.title: Plan 9 from Outer Space
films.cast: Bela Lugosi, Gregory Walcott, Mona McKinnon
films.description: Aliens threaten the world order while zombies stumble through graveyards in Ed Wood’s astonishing masterpiece, screening here from a new print made by the BFI National Archive.

tags: Trash! The Wildest Films You’ve Ever Seen

films.title: Punishment Park
films.cast: Patrick Boland, Kent Foreman, Carmon Argenziano
films.description: Presaging the age of reality television and an all-too prescient rise in authoritarian, illiberal governance, Peter Watkins’ film now feels less like a dystopian sci-fi than an it-could-happen-tomorrow scenario.

films.title: Raging Bull
films.cast: Robert De Niro, Joe Pesci, Cathy Moriarty
films.description: Martin Scorsese’s drama about the life and times of legendary middleweight boxer Jake LaMotta features a startling, Oscar-winning performance by Robert De Niro.

films.title: The Quiz of Rassilon – Doctor Who: TV Movie Special
films.description: The Quiz of Rassilon returns to the BFI for another adventure through the trivia of Doctor Who.

films.title: UK Premiere of 4K Restoration: The Razor’s Edge
films.cast: Jacques Weber, Hala Bassam, Juliet Berto, Ali Diab
films.description: A unique example of activist experimental cinema on the survival of people during a time of occupation, thanks to the power of art.

films.title: Rip It Up
films.description: Young curators and speakers lead a takeover, exhibiting work by and about young people.

films.title: Rocco and His Brothers
films.cast: Alain Delon, Annie Girardot, Katina Paxinou, Paolo Stoppa
films.description: Luchino Visconti's landmark drama, beloved by Martin Scorsese, sees themes of love, migration, struggle and boxing collide in post-war Milan.

films.title: Rocky
films.cast: Sylvester Stallone, Talia Shire, Burt Young
films.description: This Oscar-winning drama finds a journeyman boxer from Philadelphia with a surprise shot at the heavyweight world championship.

films.title: Rose of Nevada
films.cast: George MacKay, Callum Turner, Rosalind Eleazar, Mary Woodvine
films.description: Cornish auteur Mark Jenkin returns with a political folktale of two fishermen locked in a time-loop.

films.title: Rose of Nevada
films.cast: George MacKay, Callum Turner, Rosalind Eleazar, Mary Woodvine
films.description: Cornish auteur Mark Jenkin returns with a political folktale of two fishermen locked in a time-loop.

films.title: Rose of Nevada
films.cast: George MacKay, Callum Turner, Rosalind Eleazar, Mary Woodvine
films.description: Cornish auteur Mark Jenkin returns with a political folktale of two fishermen locked in a time-loop.

films.title: Rose of Nevada
films.cast: George MacKay, Callum Turner, Rosalind Eleazar, Mary Woodvine
films.description: Cornish auteur Mark Jenkin returns with a political folktale of two fishermen locked in a time-loop.

films.title: São Paulo Incorporated
films.cast: Walmor Chagas, Eva Wilma, Otelo Zeloni, Darlene Glória
films.description: Corporate success and personal collapse in a defining modernist city portrait.

films.title: Silent Dreams Shorts Programme
films.description: A collection of innovative and experimental silent shorts that revel in the world of dreams.

films.title: Small Axe: Lovers Rock
films.cast: Micheal Ward, Amarah-Jae St Aubyn
films.description: The jubilant high point of Steve McQueen’s Small Axe series recreates the blossoming romance between two people attending a house party in West London at the start of the 1980s.

films.title: Sporting Love + Marry the Girl
films.cast: Sonnie Hale, Winifred Shotter, Hugh Wakefield, Wally Patch
films.description: From the early period of British Lion and Hammer, these two rare comedies of farcical romance, weddings and tomfoolery are now only viewable on 9.5mm film.

films.title: A Star is Born
films.cast: Judy Garland, James Mason, Jack Carson, Charles Bickford
films.description: Hollywood crowns a new star while quietly burying another, in a film that features Judy Garland’s finest performance.

films.title: Super 8½
films.cast: Bruce LaBruce, Stacy Friedrich, Mikey Mike, Klaus von Brücker
films.description: A down-on-his luck porn director sees his chance to stage a comeback when he collaborates with a lesbian documentary filmmaker.

tags: Trash! The Wildest Films You’ve Ever Seen
Trash! The Wildest Films You’ve Ever Seen

films.title: Sunset Boulevard
films.cast: William Holden, Gloria Swanson, Erich von Stroheim, Nancy Olson
films.description: A venomous Hollywood elegy where fame rots, dreams linger, and a forgotten star scripts her comeback by consuming the man foolish enough to believe it.

films.title: The Thin Red Line
films.cast: Jim Caviezel, Sean Penn, Nick Nolte
films.description: Terrence Malick’s return to the screen after a two-decade absence saw him deliver an epic, yet powerfully intimate treatise on ‘war in the heart of nature’.

films.title: Surviving Earth
films.cast: Slavko Sobin, Olive Gray
films.description: In Thea Gajic’s social realist feature debut, we follow Yugoslavian refugee Vlad as he embraces a life in Bristol and battles addiction.

films.title: Thundercrack!
films.cast: Marion Eaton, George Kuchar, Melinda McDowell
films.description: One dark and stormy night, a group of strangers take refuge in a remote house. What happens next guaranteed this film a place in cinema history.

tags: Trash! The Wildest Films You’ve Ever Seen

films.title: Tales of Arcadia
films.description: We celebrate this classic animated TV show in the company of its creator, Guillermo del Toro.

films.title: Timecode Live
films.cast: Jeanne Tripplehorn, Stellan Skarsgård, Salma Hayek
films.description: Mike Figgis remixes his all-star, experimental, split screen, single-shot (times four) Hollywood-set drama.

films.title: Time Without Pity
films.cast: Michael Redgrave, Ann Todd, Leo McKern, Peter Cushing
films.description: This is a key film that Hollywood blacklistee Joseph Losey shot during his British exile.

films.title: Tip Toe
films.cast: Alan Cumming, David Morrissey
films.description: Alan Cumming and David Morrissey star in this tense suburban thriller set in Manchester.

films.title: Train of Events
films.cast: Jack Warner, Peter Finch, Valerie Hobson
films.description: One of the finest Ealing Studio portmanteau films, incorporating multiple mediums, including early references to television.

films.title: The Truman Show
films.cast: Jim Carrey, Laura Linney, Natascha McElhone, Ed Harris
films.description: Peter Weir offers a piercing portrait of a media-saturated society and highlights the touching humanity that stands in stark contrast to it.

tags: Finding Your Way: The Films of Peter Weir

films.title: Turn the Key Softly
films.cast: Yvonne Mitchell, Joan Collins, Kathleen Harrison, Terence Morgan
films.description: This gem of London films follows a trio of troubled women.

films.title: TwentyFourSeven
films.cast: Bob Hoskins, Karl Collins, James Corden, Bruce Jones
films.description: Shane Meadow’s directorial feature debut chronicles the lives of a group of working-class youths transformed through their involvement with a local boxing club.

films.title: Short Film Showcase
films.description: UK Asian Film Festival showcases ten distinctive shorts spotlighting the range and vitality of South Asian and British Asian filmmaking.

films.title: We Are Also Brothers
films.cast: Grande Otelo, Aguinaldo Camargo, Vera Nunes
films.description: Two brothers, two paths in a pioneering drama of race and social mobility.

films.title: Ways of Seeing Archives: Films by Chris Paul Daniels, Tierney Walker and Tina Grace
films.description: New and recent films stage radical re-engagements with materials held in the North West Film Archive and the John Berger archive at the British Library.

films.title: We Are Also Brothers
films.cast: Grande Otelo, Aguinaldo Camargo, Vera Nunes
films.description: Two brothers, two paths in a pioneering drama of race and social mobility.

films.title: When We Were Kings
films.description: Leon Gast’s stunning film thrillingly documents the legendary 1974 ‘Rumble in the Jungle’ fight between Muhammad Ali and George Foreman.

films.title: Witness
films.cast: Harrison Ford, Kelly McGillis, Lukas Haas, Danny Glover
films.description: Set within the quiet life of an Amish community, this unique crime film finds power in the most fundamental acts of care as it builds towards a nail-biting finale.

tags: Finding Your Way: The Films of Peter Weir

films.title: The Woman with No Name
films.cast: Phyllis Calvert, Edward Underdown, Richard Burton, Helen Cherry
films.description: In this post-war British melodrama, Phyllis Calvert stars as a young woman with amnesia who gradually uncovers her own dark past.

films.title: The Yellow Balloon
films.cast: William Sylvester, Kenneth More, Kathleen Ryan, Andrew Ray
films.description: The most thrilling entry in the cycle of ‘hunted children’ films in British cinema.

films.title: The Year of Living Dangerously
films.cast: Mel Gibson, Sigourney Weaver, Linda Hunt
films.description: Welding a searing critique of Western imperialism with a furious tale of love and desire, this lush adaptation is simply breathtaking.

tags: Finding Your Way: The Films of Peter Weir
